programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Murdered for Being Different
programme.desc: Fact-based drama telling the story of a brutal attack on a couple in a Lancashire park. Sophie Lancaster was kicked to death by a gang she didn't know, while her boyfriend Robert Maltby was beaten into a coma - and all because they were dressed as goths. Nico Mirallegro, Abigail Lawrie, Sally Lindsay and Chanel Cresswell star(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral travels from Indonesia to Singapore on the next leg of its round-the-world trip, and the Rev Colin Still gets a reminder of his past on a steam train trip in Java. Choir mistress Sue Alderson starts to drill a new group of singers to perform at Colin's Palm Sunday service, but a change of schedule in Singapore causes her problems(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: It's Easter, and the Rev Colin Still has a busy week ahead, including attending a Mission to Seafarers service in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The Balmoral also stops in Phuket, Thailand, where Colin visits a Buddhist temple and choir mistress Sue Alderson joins him for an elephant ride at a sanctuary. Ken and Shirley Marshall are celebrating their 60th wedding anniversary on the cruise and experience an exhilarating journey on a motorised rickshaw in Colombo(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Jean Johansson finds out how Longleat's giant otters Gordito and Inny are getting on, while Kate Humble helps put the European grey wolves' stamina to the test(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Taking on the Irish Sea - Our Lives
programme.desc: Following the 12-strong crew from Llangrannog rowing club in Ceredigion as they prepare and compete in sea-rowing race the Celtic Challenge across the Irish Sea from Arklow in the Republic of Ireland to Aberystwyth. The eight women and four men, including three grandmothers, a fire officer, a police inspector, a builder, a veterinary nurse, a foster carer and a student, all pull together for nearly 100 miles of open sea that can take over a day to cross. Dot Davies narrates(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v St Helens, Castleford Tigers v London Broncos and Salford Red Devils v Huddersfield Giants(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Simon King and Steve Brown for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo argue that, while convenience food serves a purpose, only the best ingredients and perfect cooking can produce something truly delicious(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Richard is called to the scene when a calf's mother collapses, while Sybil has to break some difficult news to a concerned horse owner. Plus, vets in Fife treat puppies that have eaten a potentially deadly amount of ibuprofen, and Megan deals with a chinchilla with problematic teeth. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts travel the globe in search of the most surprising animal stories and reveal how and why animals do such remarkable things. The first episode explores the world of animal intelligence, with Hugh visiting Florida to investigate an unexpected relationship between an alligator and a manatee, which has animal intelligence experts baffled. Plus, conservationist Giles Clark finds out if elephants can feel empathy - one of the most sophisticated forms of emotional intelligence(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow search for antiques in Essex and Suffolk, where newcomer Louise gambles every penny of her kitty in a valiant effort to unseat her rival(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French revolution(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the chefs to Cadiz in southern Spain, a city famous for its seafood. The brigade must cook for a restaurant that has been run by the same family for 50 years, and is adored by locals and tourists alike. Jason must encourage the chefs to appreciate local traditions, and arranges for a local fisherman to take them to the salt flats that have provided seafood to residents for thousands of years. Back in the kitchen, several members of the brigade have to win back Jason's trust following recent confrontations(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Nish Kumar, Ellie Taylor and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Documentary following more people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. They include Tyrone, a homeless teenager who works a zero-hour contract at McDonald's and wants to rent his own flat - but cannot guarantee he will earn enough each week to cover the rent. Plus, the story of Ecuadorian immigrant Angelica, who lives in London, where she studies English all day and works night shifts as a cleaner. After requesting a pay rise to the living wage, she was suspended by her employment agency(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation of a rural case of murder and moonlighting in Ireland in 1894, when James Donovan was dragged from his bed and beaten to death by two men(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: Return of the programme following people going through pivotal experiences. After developing multiple sclerosis, ex-railway worker Simon finds out if a dog can restore his independence, while entrepreneurs Charlotte and Kim risk their business in a bid to launch a new product. Dominic Littlewood narrates(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral leaves Dubai as it sets off on the last leg of its world tour, stopping briefly in Oman before heading through the Suez Canal to the Egyptian port of Alexandria. The Rev Colin Still and a group of passengers visit the Allied war cemetery at El Alamein to commemorate those that fell in the battle. On board, people come together to take part in the Country Fayre to raise money for charity, and then it's time for end-of-cruise shows by the passengers and staff, who bid tearful goodbyes at the end of the four-month voyage(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet two endangered Cuban crocodiles, while keepers and vets are battling to get to the bottom of why rare a red panda is not putting on weight(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In Kenya, new-born elephant calf Safina learns how to keep up with her fast moving herd, and in Sri Lanka, one-week-old Jazir has to quickly learn the ropes of a primate society. At the edge of the Arctic, five-week-old fox cubs must learn to hunt before their siblings beat them to limited food, and in Uganda, an eight-week-old mountain gorilla adapts to forest life(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: The first build on offer is in Stretford, where Karen wants to convert her cellar into a playroom for her train-obsessed stepson on a budget of £1,500. In Woking, Ross and Giovanna's bedroom hasn't been updated in decades and is need of a complete refit, including an entire wall of bespoke wardrobes(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman has his first taste of Korean barbecue food and samples Swedish cuisine, and Ainsley Harriott cooks a salmon and mango salad inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the nation's love affair with beer and cider, revealing how at one time it was safer to drink ale than water. The duo are also given a chance to gain hands-on brewing experience at a cider festival(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Rosie treats a concussed tawny owl, while equine specialist Kerry helps a horse with a badly cut face. Meanwhile, in Fife, Adam has an emotional case on his hands when an owner suffering from a debilitating phobia discovers the dog he depends on has a worrying lump. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on animals' relationships with each other and with humans. Hugh discovers how the capuchin monkeys of Costa Rica have such a fantastic team spirit, biologist Patrick Aryee investigates man's relationship with the vulture - nature's most infamous scavenger - and zoologist Lucy Cooke finds out about the medicine in Kentucky that may improve a rare pig's relationship problems. Conservationist Giles Clark ventures into the rainforests of Thailand to find out how a clever matchmaking technique could help save the clouded leopard from extinction(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Phil Serrell and Louise Gostelow head to Lincolnshire to search for antiques, where they come across an antique globe and a very rare King Charles Spaniel(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter Zoe Salmon, medical expert Dr Sara Kayat, comedian Shazia Mirza, former Team GB runner Iwan Thomas and star of Channel 4's The Hotel Mark Jenkins set out to win a cash prize for their charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a mirror and indicator for cyclists, healthy baking mixes and a 3D noticeboard. But only one can win the Customer is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. Presented by Fearne Cotton. In the first episode, the ten contestants are split into two teams and must redesign two show homes, working together to deliver a cohesive end product that impresses the judges and is accessible to prospective buyers(n)

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: With access to Police Scotland's Major Investigations Teams (MITs), this series goes behind the scenes on the most complex murder and large-scale criminal investigations. This episode follows the investigation into the disappearance and subsequent murder of 47-year-old Julie Reilly, who went missing from her Govan flat in March 2018(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Phyllis is behaving more eratically and becoming more confused when she is hospitalised. Contains some strong language. Comedy drama, starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the chefs to Cadiz in Southern Spain, a city famous for its seafood. The brigade must cook for a restaurant that has been run by the same family for 50 years, and is adored by locals and tourists alike. Jason must encourage the chefs to appreciate local traditions, and arranges for a local fisherman to take them to the salt flats that have provided seafood to residents for thousands of years. Back in the kitchen, several members of the brigade have to win back Jason's trust following recent confrontations(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Documentary following traders at a Sunday market A new couple have an unusual product to sell, and the market crew puts on a fashion show(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: A wolf cub is seriously injured by the rest of the pack in Wolf Wood. Meanwhile, keepers attempt to catch some of the meerkats to give them a dose of medicine(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland and Northern Ireland as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn Cassells and Sandra Baer face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie Mitchell's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, hill farmer Johnny Irvine and his son Raymond are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee Budge from Shetland puts her pony skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britain's famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out 'who ate all the pies' on the football pitch(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Farm vet Richard carries out a tracheotomy to save a calf's life at Teesdale dairy farm, while in Fife, Megan investigates the cause of a guinea pig's cough and needs to remove a stick that is lodged inside a dog. Elsewhere, Steve is determined to help an abandoned elderly cat find a new home. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on the strange world of animal communication, including scientists who are interpreting the howl of the wolf. Lucy Cooke discovers the communication skills required to persuade a penguin to swim and also heads to the Bahamas, where new research claims to have found that sharks could be social, make friendships and even have personalities. Conservationist Giles Clark is in Australia to see how technology is saving the koala and biologist Patrick Aryee is in South Africa to investigate the seductive calls of a lovestruck lioness(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Phil Serrell and Louise Gostelow scour the emporiums of Lincolnshire. Phil buys something rusty, while Louise is faced with a puzzle. Then they begin the bidding war at auction in Battle, East Sussex(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Doctors stars Chris Walker, Matthew Chambers, Sarah Moyle, Ian Midlane and Elisabeth Dermot Walsh take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score an anti-snoring ring, a pet brush and massager and a car-cleaning kit. But only one can win the Customer is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. Master saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher tackles the repair of a worn out and badly torn leather pouffe with precious memories for its owner Lou, silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a battered silver purse and mechanical guru Steve Fletcher is faced with how to carry out the delicate repair of an old Victorian rain gauge(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond and a team of volunteers give the valais blacknose sheep a bath, Kirsty and Tom rescue fallen sheep on the isle, and Stevie attempts to milk his first buffalo. In Shetland, Kirsty and Tom again row out to St Ninian's Isle to attempt to rescue a ewe and a lamb that have fallen from the isle's steep cliffs(n)

programme.title: Murder Case

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the area around the summit of Snowdon in summer, finding buzzards nesting and moths being hunted by migrant nightjars from Africa. He also climbs Cadair Idris, the highest peak in the south part of the park, visits sand dunes full of orchids and spends a day on the moors watching the hen harrier.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the smartphone, discovering how the Roman army sent messages over long distances using fire and examining the advances made by Samuel Morse, Alexander Graham Bell's favour and Guglielmo Marconi. The programme also examines how the inventions of scientist and movie star Hedy Lamarr has been used to increase network capacity(n)

programme.title: The Rise and Fall of Nokia Mobile
programme.desc: How the Finnish mobile phone company's global domination was created and then eroded, told by those that lived and worked through the rollercoaster years at Nokia(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Investigating the story of how a Russian internet millionaire, Dmitry Itskov, is turning to cutting-edge science to try to unlock the secret of living for ever. The programme investigates the real science inspiring his bold plan to upload the human mind to a computer, and examines whether his goal of bringing about immortality for humans within thirty years is attainable(n)

programme.title: The Somme: Secret Tunnel Wars
programme.desc: Documentary following historian Peter Barton and a team of archaeologists as they navigate a network of tunnels constructed beneath the Somme battlefield during the First World War. The programme unearths the history embedded in the underground labyrinth - facilitated by both the Allied and Central Powers to plant mines beyond enemy lines - and the personal stories of the predominantly British troops who helped dig it during the first two years of the conflict(n)

programme.title: Battlefield Britain
programme.desc: The Snows use state-of-the-art graphics to re-create the Battle of Britain, one of the pivotal conflicts of the Second World War. Peter explains the role of radar in detecting Luftwaffe planes as they headed for Dover, while Dan experiences the physiological effects of the high G-forces that would have been felt by the RAF pilots(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Eisteddfod 2019 with Jason Mohammad
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter reports from Llanrwst in the Conwy Valley, bringing highlights and stories from the National Eisteddfod of Wales. He catches up with Welsh rugby stars Jonathan Davies and Ken Owens, as well as broadcaster and comedian Tudur Owen, who are all being honoured at the Eisteddfod this year(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Historian Neil Oliver explores the roles played by Alexander II, who forged the country through blood and violence, and William Wallace, a patriot whose resistance to King Edward of England inspired Scottish people to fight for their independence(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George II's family fought the French, the Jacobites and one another at the same time. She examines his troubles with his son Frederick, Prince of Wales, and the way he led from the front on the battlefield, becoming the last British monarch to do so, and helped turn the country into a global superpower(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: Scholar Mary Beard tackles one of the biggest puzzles of all as she examines how and when the Roman Empire fell, surveying the walls and fortifications of Britain and Germany. She seeks to redefine understanding of so-called 'Barbarian Invasions', before ultimately asking whether the Empire was transformed rather than destroyed, and still lives on in the world today(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The comedian introduces highlights from the Edinburgh Fringe, showcasing some of this year's best new talent. She chats to special guests including Frank Skinner, Larry Dean, Iain Stirling and Desiree Burch, and introduces performances by Jo Caulfield and Stuart Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Timeshift: The Last Days of Steam
programme.desc: Between 1948 and 1960, governments around the world invested in electric tracks and diesel, but in Britain hundreds of steam-powered trains were built. This film looks at the reasons behind their continued production, and asks what happened to them(n)

programme.title: Calculating Ada: The Countess of Computing
programme.desc: Hannah Fry tells the story of Ada Lovelace, the daughter of Lord Byron and a scandalous socialite. But when Charles Babbage designed the world's first steam-powered computers, most famously the analytical engine, it was Ada who realised the full potential of these machines(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as the European Space Agency selects its next mission, which will be launched in 2028, meeting British teams vying to have their ideas selected, including a revolutionary mission to a comet(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: With several recent tragedies at air shows, two former RAF pilots - the Wildcats - must finish their display season safely. Meanwhile, a champion skydiver juggles motherhood with an intense training schedule, and a man attempts to fly Britain's first solar balloon(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Clare Balding travels to South Africa to join a veterinary team trying to help a rhino called Thandi who was attacked by poachers for her horn. For the first time, a plastic surgeon will attempt a skin graft on the animal to heal the wound. She also visits the rainforest of Borneo to follow the story of an orangutan who needs cataracts removing from her eyes so that she can be released back into the wild. Elsewhere, vet Steve Leonard takes a trip to Chicago's Lincoln Park Zoo, where the latest technology is being used to help chimpanzees with heart disease and visits the South African city of Pretoria, where a Cape fur seal requires a CT scan to identify the root cause of her illness, before witnessing a giraffe being anaesthetised to treat a leg injury(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: The professor explores one of Saqqara's last pyramid complexes to illustrate how Ancient Egypt's 'Pyramid Age' came to an end. A worsening climate combined with political upheaval, famine and economic difficulties to plunge the state into a dark era of civil war, with the land dividing into smaller city-states headed by ambitious small-town leaders. In an obscure tomb in Thebes, she uncovers the stories of warriors who fought in the bloody battle that would eventually lead to Egypt's reunification, and reveals how settlers known as the 'Hyksos' tried to infiltrate the fledgling nation's government to seize the throne(n)

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Hosted by Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia anxiously waits to hear news of Bex's A-level results and Stuart makes a desperate plea to win Mick back as a friend, but his words fall on deaf ears. After an unsettling conversation with Iqra, Honey tells Will and Janet to pack their things, while Iqra apologises for lying to Mariam and Arshad(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Keeping Faith

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: The Warwick Uni Rape Chat Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

A trial week in Melbourne sees them explore the properties, work opportunities and lifestyle on offer. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Murdered for Being Different
programme.desc: Fact-based drama telling the story of a brutal attack on a couple in a Lancashire park. programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Florida woman Jennifer Mee who, as a teenager, became a media sensation when she suffered a particularly strange ailment - a persistent case of hiccups. But in 2010, she lured a man to a dark alleyway where her friends were waiting to rob him. Instead he ended up being shot dead. Even though she didn't pull the trigger, and was nowhere near when the bullets were fired, Jennifer was sentenced to life without parole. Piers charts the journey through fame and rejection that saw Jennifer descend into a criminal lifestyle, and asks family members and legal experts if they believe her punishment was too harsh(n)

Over-stretched, working mother Lisa never lives up to the expectations she sets herself, and cannot help but compare herself to her female friends, Kate and Roz. Behind closed doors however, each of the women struggle with the pressures to keep up the image of having the 'perfect life'. Starring Anna Friel, Rosalind Eleazar and Sinead Keenan(n)

programme.desc: An insight into the auctions where criminals' assets from their nefarious activities are sold at auction, with the proceeds usually going to help those whose lives have been blighted by crime. programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Florida woman Jennifer Mee who, as a teenager, became a media sensation when she suffered a particularly strange ailment - a persistent case of hiccups. But in 2010, she lured a man to a dark alleyway where her friends were waiting to rob him. Instead he ended up being shot dead. Even though she didn't pull the trigger, and was nowhere near when the bullets were fired, Jennifer was sentenced to life without parole. Piers charts the journey through fame and rejection that saw Jennifer descend into a criminal lifestyle, and asks family members and legal experts if they believe her punishment was too harsh(n)

programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. programme.title: Speed Freaks

programme.title: Enter the Dragon
programme.desc: A powerful crime kingpin hosts a martial arts tournament on his island lair. A former Shaolin monk is recruited by an intelligence agency to enter the tournament and expose the crime lord's opium and slavery rings. He finds himself fighting for his life alongside two Vietnam veterans who are both fleeing their own enemies. Thriller, starring Bruce Lee, John Saxon, Jim Kelly and Kien Shih(n)

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle begin an investigation into why a city's crime rate is steadily falling. They later find themselves victimised by local officers after being charged with a minor traffic offence. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A villain comes out of hiding in an attempt to retrieve stolen loot and only Regan and Carter can stop him. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade ventures into Brazil's cattle country as he searches for a deadly electric eel that has killed three cowboys. To catch his elusive prey he has to combat all his fears, and after a long chase he finally corners it in an unexpected place(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits Africa's Rift Valley on a quest to track down the Nile perch, a fish that can grow to a length of more than 8ft and weigh 500lbs. Facing dangers such as crocodile attacks, rival fishing gangs and hippos capable of mauling people to death, he embarks on possibly his most perilous mission yet(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel across Italy, with their main target a monster wels catfish of up to 300lbs in the River Po. They also aim to catch smaller channel catfish from the River Arno and carp from a stunning mountain lake near Bologna(n)

programme.title: Licence to Kill
programme.desc: James Bond plans revenge on a drug baron who maimed his friend. However, when the British government refuses to take official action, 007 finds himself stripped of his licence to kill and forced to go it alone without the backing of MI6. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro(n)

The second edition focuses on the decisions Jade Goody made which proved disastrous for her life in the spotlight, including a stint on Celebrity Big Brother which left her reputation and business in ruins after a series of abusive rows with Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty triggered a huge wave of complaints about racist bullying(n)

programme.title: The Town the Gypsies Took Over
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Britain's oldest and most exclusive Gypsy gathering, the Appleby Horse Fair in Cumbria, which is attended by more than 10,000 travellers. The programme explores how new police restrictions are impacting on the festival's traditions, as well as the disruption caused by torrential rain, including a nearby river rising to a dangerous level. Plus, local business owners share their thoughts on the event. While some stand to make a profit from the visitors, others find the size of the fair overwhelming(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Each stone bears hieroglyphic symbols that have yet to be completely deciphered(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Manor Moat
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the experts head to Llancaiach Fawr Manor, near Caerphilly, south Wales, to investigate an ancient moat. The team's geophysicists feel the site should provide them with the ideal conditions to determine what the ditch was originally designed to guard - but the project soon becomes one of the most baffling investigations in the programme's history(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: The Hunt for Hitler's Scientists
programme.desc: An examination of the Nazi weapons salvaged by the Allies at the end of the Second World War, and how the acquisition of enemy technology influenced future military campaigns(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate Gateholm Island, off the coast of Pembrokeshire, where a number of mysterious artefacts were discovered some time ago, suggesting the site was once of religious importance. The only way to get to the island is by rigging a 500-metre zip wire way above the wave-lashed rocks. The archaeologists battle inclement weather to begin a dig on the mainland, where team leader Francis Pryor sees characteristics suggestive of a classic Iron Age fort beginning to emerge(n)

programme.title: Time Team

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Extreme World
programme.desc: Ross Kemp visits the Central American republic of Honduras, where he makes his way to the city of San Pedro Sula. With a rate of 169 homicides per 100,000 inhabitants last year - an average of more than three people each day - Sula has the dubious distinction of being the most dangerous city on the planet outside of a war zone. Ross discovers how armed drug gangs have taken over many parts of the country, forcing thousands to flee north on a freight train that transports migrants through Guatemala and across Mexico into the United States. But it's an arduous journey and not for the faint-hearted. Even if passengers avoid falling off the roof of the train to their deaths, they face being robbed by bandits, raped, murdered or kidnapped and sold into slavery(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The team returns to investigate more health stories, with Chris van Tulleken examining the intriguing idea that the way we prepare food could be damaging our health. Okay, so we all know that frying isn't as good for us as steaming, but a radical new experiment suggests that the health-conscious among us could be cutting calories by making simple adjustments to the way we prepare starchy foods. in fact, certain methods could make them healthier than anyone previously imagined. Plus, surgeon Gabriel Weston witnesses a new procedure giving new hope to paralysed people in America, and journalist Michael Mosley looks at evidence that suggests we should all be spending a sensible amount of time in the sun(n)

programme.desc: Three Russian aristocrats - flighty Natasha, pacifist Pierre and military officer Andrei - endure a series of tumultuous events as Naopleon Bonaparte's armies invade their country. While Andrei is sent to the front line, Pierre is drawn into a loveless marriage, ignorant of Natasha's feelings for him. Historical drama based on Leo Tolstoy's novel, starring Audrey Hepburn, Henry Fonda and John Mills(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
